items.title: Agents think in milliseconds, legacy infrastructure doesn't. LinkedIn, Walmart and Zendesk shared how they closed the gap at VB Transform 2026
items.summary: Legacy infrastructure, not the models themselves, is what's actually slowing AI agents down. items.title: Brex built its AI agent policy by watching what agents actually do, not by writing rules first
items.summary: OpenClaw has become one of the most widely adopted agentic frameworks, but it has yet to prove itself at enterprise scale. Agents need real credentials — API keys, OAuth tokens, service accounts — to work effectively, and Brex found that traditional guardrails couldn't contain what those agents were
items.author: taryn.plumb@venturebeat.com (Taryn Plumb)

items.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. items.author: VentureBeat
